text: Welcome to Iom Hey Anonymous, We hope this report finds you in good health and positive spirit. We're excited to guide you on the journey of discovery and improvement. In this personalized report, you'll get a clear snapshot of your gut health at the time of sampling. We have analyzed how your microbiome affects your Bloating, Constipation, Diarrhea and Stress and included tailored food recommendations to help you feel better. Our team of experts is here to support you every step of the way. To dive deeper into your results, we invite you to book a one-on-one session with a specialist who’ll walk you Let’s partner with your through your insights and help you apply them to your daily microbiome and take a life. step toward better health Warm regards, —together. Team Iom

Your Microbiome 02 Overview We craft a microbiome profile, outlining its influence on key health aspects, rooted in the initial bacterial snapshot. Gut Diversity Gut diversity is the range of different bacteria living in your gut, showing how balanced and healthy your digestive ecosystem is.Low Low gut diversity means fewer bacterial species and weaker microbiome function. This can increase the risk of inflammation, metabolic issues, and infections. F/B Ratio The Firmicutes to Bacteroidetes (F/B) ratio shows the balance between two major groups of gut bacteria that help regulate digestion and metabolism.A high Firmicutes to Bacteroidetes (F/B) High ratio means Firmicutes dominate.This may increase calorie extraction from food, contributing to weight gain and metabolic strain. Name: Anonymous | Date: 10-11-2025 | Iom ID: IOM_KIT001

Pathogens & 06 Probiotic Presence Pathogens Some microbes in your gut may be less friendly — but understanding them is the first step toward balance. Bacteria Abundance Level Escherichia-Shigella 0.22% Low Enterobacter 0.09% Low Staphylococcus 0.05% Low Klebsiella 0.04% Low Enterococcus 0.03% Low Haemophilus 0.01% Low Legends: Great % Good % Bad % What This Means for You Many pathogens are regular residents of a normal gut. The presence of these pathogens does not necessarily indicate the presence of disease. Any condition will be defined by the nature of the pathogen, your overall health and other conditions. This is not a diagnostic report. Name: Anonymous | Date: 10-11-2025 | Iom ID: IOM_KIT001

Gut Heal Bacteria 09 to Decrease Your Gut Now Your Optimised Gut Bifidobacterium Blautia Lachnospira Dorea Roseburia Other IBS bacteria to decrease Bifidobacterium Current: 10.8% Optimised: 10.77% These probiotic, immunity enhancing bacteria live in your intestines and help body fight diarrhea, constipation, and other intestinal disorders.By lowering its levels and supporting other beneficial microbes, we promote a healthier gut environment for smoother digestion and overall wellness. Blautia Current: 2.48% Optimised: 2.35% Blautia is a butyrate producer; supposed to have a probiotic effect on the body, Blautia contributes to regulating health by reducing metabolic disorders.Toning down an overgrowth of Blautia can help ease bloating and keep your gut feeling more comfortable. Name: Anonymous | Date: 10-11-2025 | Iom ID: IOM_KIT001

Gut Heal Bacteria 10 to Decrease Lachnospira Current: 0.22% Optimised: 0.15% Lachnospira is a fiber-fermenting gut bacterium that supports digestive health and produces beneficial short-chain fatty acids like butyrate.Their overgrowth may contribute to anxiety, so maintaining balance is important and hence it is being reduced. Dorea Current: 0.17% Optimised: 0.12% This sweet sounding bacteria behaves as per its surrounding bacteria and nutrients. Mostly it is involved in degrading the mucin in the gut and it is seen that Dorea aids your digestion.High Dorea levels may contribute to bloating and loose stools. Reducing its abundance can restore balance, ease digestive discomfort, and promote comfortable bowel movements. Roseburia Current: 0.15% Optimised: 0.1% Thse are part of bacteria producing short-chain fatty acids, especially butyrate, affecting colonic motility, immunity, anti-inflammatory properties and a lean biomarker built-in.Lowering this bacteria in your gut is part of our optimization strategy, which carefully balances it alongside other beneficial microbes to promote a healthier, more comfortable digestive system. Name: Anonymous | Date: 10-11-2025 | Iom ID: IOM_KIT001

Gut Heal Bacteria 11 to Increase Your Gut Now Your Optimised Gut Dialister Faecalibacterium Catenibacterium Subdoligranulum Collinsella Other IBS bacteria to increase Dialister Current: 5.08% Optimised: 6.6% Dialister often increases in people with fatigue and IBS, while low levels are linked to constipation, suggesting it helps keep digestion moving.Having moderate levels of Dialister in your gut may help improve digestion, support smoother bowel movements, and even reduce gut discomfort. Faecalibacterium Current: 2.95% Optimised: 3.84% A good bacteria with a prebiotic impact on your gut, Faecalibacterium is an important fella that produces butyrate, and propionate, maintains gut equilibrium, and good gut health.Supporting and maintaining this beneficial microbe can help reduce inflammation, ease discomfort, and promote overall gut health. Name: Anonymous | Date: 10-11-2025 | Iom ID: IOM_KIT001

Gut Heal Bacteria 11 to Increase Catenibacterium Current: 2.11% Optimised: 2.74% Catenibacterium is a gut bacteria that ferments carbohydrates to produce SCFAs. When present in higher amounts, it’s linked to more regular stool frequency, helping keep digestion smooth and comfortable.Increasing Catenibacterium levels can help relieve constipation, thanks to the SCFAs it produces that support healthy gut motility. Subdoligranulum Current: 0.8% Optimised: 1.04% The function of the gut bacterium Subdoligranulum is complex and still being researched, with both beneficial and potentially harmful roles identified. As a notable producer of butyrate, it is generally associated with better gut health.It is being increased because it is a top butyrate producer, fueling colon cells, keeping the gut barrier strong, and calming inflammation. Collinsella Current: 0.48% Optimised: 0.62% An interesting bacteria commonly seen in the human gut, Collinsella is often a marker for the metabolic condition of the body. The bacteria ferments plant and animal origin carbs to produce lactate that converts to energy. It also plays a role in processing bile acids and can influence gas production, which may affect digestion and symptoms like bloating.A moderate increase in Collinsella can help support healthy lipid metabolism, contributing to better fat processing and overall metabolic balance. Name: Anonymous | Date: 10-11-2025 | Iom ID: IOM_KIT001
